heading: Certification and judicial review

children.label: A
children.text: whether the State has established a mechanism for the appointment, compensation, and payment of reasonable litigation expenses of competent counsel in State postconviction proceedings brought by indigent prisoners who have been sentenced to death;

children.label: B
children.text: the date on which the mechanism described in subparagraph (A) was established; and

children.label: C
children.text: whether the State provides standards of competency for the appointment of counsel in proceedings described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—Effective date
children.label: 2 .—Effective date
children.text: The date the mechanism described in paragraph (1)(A) was established shall be the effective date of the certification under this subsection.

children.heading: .—Only express requirements
children.label: 3 .—Only express requirements
children.text: There are no requirements for certification or for application of this chapter other than those expressly stated in this chapter.

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: b .—Regulations
children.text: The Attorney General shall promulgate regulations to implement the certification procedure under subsection (a).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The determination by the Attorney General regarding whether to certify a State under this section is subject to review exclusively as provided under chapter 158 of this title.

children.heading: .—Venue
children.label: 2 .—Venue
children.text: The Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit shall have exclusive jurisdiction over matters under paragraph (1), subject to review by the Supreme Court under .section 2350 of this title

children.heading: .—Standard of review
children.label: 3 .—Standard of review
children.text: The determination by the Attorney General regarding whether to certify a State under this section shall be subject to de novo review.
